steps.title: Graph the weight interval
steps.content: The calf's weight after $n$ days is $5900+250n$. In Desmos, graph the weight line with the two cutoff weights. The integer $x$-values where the line is between the two horizontal lines are the possible day counts.

steps.title: Use the intersections as bounds
steps.content: The line reaches $8650$ pounds when $5900+250n=8650$, so $250n=2750$ and $n=11$. It reaches $9650$ pounds when $5900+250n=9650$, so $250n=3750$ and $n=15$.

steps.title: Choose the integer days inside the interval
steps.content: The weight must be greater than $8650$ and less than $9650$, so $n$ must be an integer strictly between $11$ and $15$. The possible values are $12$, $13$, and $14$.

steps.title: Check a valid entry
steps.content: For example, $n=12$ gives $5900+250(12)=8900$, and $8650\lt8900\lt9650$. Therefore any of $12$, $13$, or $14$ is a valid entry.
